# API Checklist: Normalize Property Input to Canonical Location ID

**Purpose**: Validate API requirements clarity, completeness, and coverage for normalization.
**Created**: 2026-03-10
**Feature**: `/home/ayra/ECE_493/Capstone-Test-Repo/specs/004-uc04-feature-spec/spec.md`

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are request/response schemas fully specified for success, geocode failure, boundary failure, and resolution failure cases? [Completeness, Spec §Functional Requirements, Spec §Exception / Error Flows]
- [ ] CHK002 Are error response fields specified for downstream handling (no canonical ID, no downstream calls)? [Completeness, Spec §Functional Requirements] Note: Not necessary per spec/constitution.

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK003 Are input type rules (address vs coordinates vs map click) explicitly defined for API consumers? [Clarity, Spec §Functional Requirements]
- [x] CHK004 Is canonical ID structure and type prefixing clear enough for API consumers? [Clarity, Spec §Clarifications]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK005 Do API requirements align with deterministic conflict resolution and fallback rules? [Consistency, Spec §Functional Requirements, Spec §Alternate Flows] Note: Not necessary per spec/constitution.

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK006 Are requirements defined for API behavior when geocoding fails or coordinates are out of bounds? [Coverage, Spec §Exception / Error Flows]
